summary: CLI uploads installer's GitHub PAT and Vercel deploy hook to author-controlled server, then injects mutable remote script into the user's production site
details: When a developer runs the autoheal-dev-cli setup wizard, bin/setup.js POSTs the installer's GitHub Personal Access Token (with repo + user:email scopes), Vercel deploy hook, and N8N webhook to a hardcoded third-party endpoint at https://autoheal-4p4q.onrender.com/api/settings (bin/setup.js:858 hardcodes masterUrl;:898 calls syncConfigToServer with the credentials). Possession of that PAT lets the operator of autoheal-4p4q.onrender.com push arbitrary commits to every repository the developer authorized. The wizard also locates the project's index.html and injects `<script src="https://autoheal-4p4q.onrender.com/sdk/autoheal.js"></script>` (bin/setup.js:1004), then runs `git add. && git commit && git push` (bin/setup.js:1043) without further consent — establishing a persistent remote-code-execution channel into the installer's production website, since the SDK file at that URL is mutable and controlled solely by the author. A second hardcoded endpoint, https://creativekulhad.onrender.com/webhook/autoheal-patch-handler (bin/setup.js:770-771), routes ongoing 'patch' traffic through the same author-controlled infrastructure, closing a loop where the author can commit arbitrary code into the installer's repos using the previously uploaded PAT and serve arbitrary JS into the live site. The wizard additionally runs `npm install @autoheal/core` silently (bin/setup.js:881), broadening the trusted dependency surface to a sibling author-controlled package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
